content.label: Cyclic integration by parts
content.applies_when: neither e^x nor sin x terminates under repeated differentiation, so after applying parts twice the original integral I reappears and you solve for it algebraically

content.label: Integration by parts
content.symbolic: integral u dv = u*v - integral v du

content.concept: u = sin x then u = cos x, dv = e^x dx
content.meaning: first pass u = sin x, dv = e^x dx (du = cos x dx, v = e^x); second pass u = cos x, dv = e^x dx

content.label: Solve algebraically for the recurring integral I
content.applies_when: the second application reproduces the original integral: I = e^x sin x - (e^x cos x + I)
content.transformation: collect I on one side: 2I = e^x sin x - e^x cos x, so I = (e^x (sin x - cos x))/2 + C

content.label: First application of parts
content.action: let I = integral e^x sin x dx; parts with u = sin x, dv = e^x dx gives I = e^x sin x - integral e^x cos x dx
content.purpose: expose the companion integral of e^x cos x

content.label: Second application of parts
content.action: apply parts to integral e^x cos x dx = e^x cos x + integral e^x sin x dx = e^x cos x + I
content.purpose: reproduce the original integral I so it can be solved for

content.label: Solve for I
content.action: substitute back to get I = e^x sin x - e^x cos x - I, then solve 2I = e^x sin x - e^x cos x
content.purpose: isolate I to finish without integrating further
